2606.18033.title: When English Isn't the Best Teacher: Source Language Effects in Cross-Lingual In-Context Learning
2606.18033.paper_summary: Cross-lingual transfer in multilingual NLP has been widely explored in supervised fine-tuning contexts, where factors like data availability and linguistic similarity largely determine transfer quality. As the field shifts toward few-shot In-Context Learning (ICL), it is often presumed that insights from fine-tuning carry over unchanged. Yet this assumption has not been rigorously evaluated, leaving open the question of how to choose source languages for cross-lingual ICL. We conduct a broad empirical study of cross-lingual transfer in ICL spanning seven tasks, six models, and a typologically diverse set of languages. We further analyze language confusion, a key obstacle for generative tasks in cross-lingual ICL. Our results show that conventional fine-tuning-based expectations do not consistently apply in the ICL regime and point to alternative heuristics for selecting source languages effectively.
2606.18033.paper_summary_zh: 跨語言轉移在多語言自然語言處理中已廣泛探討，特別是在監督式微調的背景下，其中數據可用性和語言相似性等因素在很大程度上決定了轉移質量。隨著該領域向少量示例的上下文學習（ICL）轉變，通常假設微調中的見解不會改變地延續下來。然而，這一假設尚未經過嚴格評估，這使得如何選擇跨語言ICL的源語言成為一個未解的問題。我們對跨語言轉移在ICL中的廣泛實證研究涵蓋了七個任務、六個模型以及一組類型學上多樣的語言。我們進一步分析了語言混淆，這是跨語言ICL中生成任務的一個主要障礙。我們的結果顯示，傳統基於微調的預期在ICL範疇內並不總是一致適用，並指出了有效選擇源語言的替代性啟發式方法。
2606.18033.author: Fred Philippy et.al.
2606.18033.authors: Fred Philippy, Siwen Guo, Jacques Klein, Tegawendé F. Bissyandé
